questions.question: Find the slope of a line parallel to $y = 3x + 2$.
A rhythm pattern has a frequency of $5 \cdot 10^2$ Hz. If a parallel pattern has the same slope, what is its frequency?
What is the equation of a line parallel to $y = 2x - 1$ and passing through $(1, 3)$?
Two parallel lines have slopes of $m_1$ and $m_2$. If $m_1 = 2$, what is $m_2$?
A canvas has a width of $15$ inches and a length of $20$ inches. If a parallel line to the length has a slope of $m$, what is $m$?
What is the relationship between two parallel lines with equations $y = 2x + 1$ and $y = 2x - 1$?
Two parallel lines intersect a line with equation $y = -2x + 1$. If the first line has equation $y = 2x - 1$, what is the equation of the second line?
A song has a rhythm pattern with a frequency of $2 \cdot 10^3$ Hz and a parallel pattern with the same slope. What is the equation of the line representing the parallel pattern?
Prove that two lines with slopes $m_1$ and $m_2$ are parallel if and only if $m_1 = m_2$. Use a musical instrument as an example.

A painter creates a canvas with a width of $20$ inches and a length of $30$ inches. A parallel line to the length has a slope of $m$. Find the equation of the line and explain how it relates to the rhythm pattern of a song.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Use unique scenarios and numerical values for each question.
Tip 2: Ensure all math is wrapped in $...$ and use \ for LaTeX commands.
Tip 3: Keep questions and options concise to fit on one page.
